R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% dplyr::rename(y = sex, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island)

fit = lapreg(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1, data = test_data, family = "bernoulli_logistic", prior_scale = 100) # large prior scale -> results should be similar to MLE
compare = glm(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1, data = prepare_data(parse_formula(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1), test_data), family = binomial())
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% dplyr::rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex) |> dplyr::mutate(y = ifelse(y > 5000, 5000, y))

fit = lapreg(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1, data = test_data, prior_scale = 100, family = "right_censored_linear") # large prior scale -> results should be similar to MLE
compare = censReg::censReg(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1, right = 5000, data = prepare_data(parse_formula(y ~ x1 + x2 + f1), test_data))
plot.lapreg(fit, group = "f1", x = "x1")

plot.lapreg(fit, group = "f1")

plot.lapreg(fit)

plot.lapreg(fit, x = "x1")
